questions.question: A single narrative detailing one of Buddha's former existences is known by which of the following names?
The Bhimbetka rock shelters, known for prehistoric cave paintings, are located in which of the following states of India?
In the Pallava administration, which was the smallest administrative unit directly managed by local self-governing assemblies?
The Parthians originated from which of the following countries?
The first systematisation of the Jain canonical literature took place at ____.
The Lion Capital located at Sarnath, which is adopted as the national emblem of the Republic of India, was erected by which of the following Mauryan kings?
Which of the following is INCORRECT about the Harappans?
questions.option_1: They exported ornaments, timber, some objects of daily use.
questions.option_2: The most favoured ornaments were beads of carnelian.
questions.option_3: The Harappans were engaged in active trade.
questions.option_4: The Harappans used only gold to make tools, pots and pans.

questions.question: During the Satavahanas kingdom, what did the terms ‘ahara’ or ‘rashtra’ refer to?
Who were the Voharikas in the judicial structure of the Magadha period?
The Eran stone inscription states that Samudragupta was chosen as king by his father because of his ______.
The Shaka dynasty in India was founded by _____.
Which of the following is NOT a principle of Jainism?
questions.option_1: Rejection of the doctrine of Karma
questions.option_2: Practice of non-injury to living beings
questions.option_3: The whole world, including stone and water, has life.
questions.option_4: Monastic life is the only way to salvation.

questions.question: Which of the following Vedas mentions the names of some learned ladies like Viswavara, Apala and Ghosa who composed mantras and attained the rank of Rishis?
What feature of Indus Valley town planning indicates advanced civic amenities and engineering skills of the civilisation?
Which of the following is a Buddhist text containing stories related to the previous births of the Buddha?
The Harappans were first in Eurasia to grow ___.
The title Devaputra, used by the Kushana rulers, implies which of the following?
questions.option_4: Divine origin or claim to divinity

questions.question: Which Gupta emperor was known as the 'Napoleon of India' due to his military conquests and strategic warfare?
Which of the following Vedic words literally means search for cows and also refers to battle?
Microliths, which are tiny stone tools usually stuck on to handles of bones or woods to make tools like saws and sickles. These were mostly found in which age?
Which of the following Buddhist texts is often called the 'Gita of Buddhism' for its importance and teachings?
The Vardhan dynasty, was also known by which of the following names?
The Acheulian cultural tradition is primarily identified with which types of advanced and symmetrical stone tools?
The term gomat in the Rigveda refers to which of the following?
Which of the following officials toured Mauryan provinces every five years to inspect administration and submit reports on provincial governance?
